title: Undercut/Overcut Opportunity Features From Race-Lap Relative State
summary: F1 strategy sources emphasize that pit timing depends not just on tyre age but on whether fresh tyres can unlock an undercut, whether traffic makes an overcut preferable, and whether pit-loss windows are open.
rationale: F1 strategy sources emphasize that pit timing depends not just on tyre age but on whether fresh tyres can unlock an undercut, whether traffic makes an overcut preferable, and whether pit-loss windows are open. That suggests race-lap relative features, not just absolute degradation thresholds.
implementation_hint: Within each race-year and lap, compute leakage-safe relative ranks or robust z-scores for LapTime_Delta, Position, TyreLife, and degradation using only same-lap competitors. Add features for local traffic pressure such as position bucket, whether pace is worse than same-lap peers on same compound, and whether the row sits in an 'undercut-open' state where current pace loss exceeds a compound-specific fresh-tyre gain proxy.
expected_effect: Should lift discrimination for midfield strategy calls where pitting depends on competitive context rather than intrinsic tyre wear alone.
risk: Same-lap aggregates can leak if computed using future rows from validation folds; track-specific effects may make generic undercut proxies noisy.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
